title: Course Schedule II
problem_statement: Return a valid ordering of courses given prerequisites, or an empty array if no order exists.

examples.explanation: Courses 0 must be taken first, then 1 and 2 before 3.

intuition: Perform topological sort on the directed graph of prerequisites to detect cycles and determine order.

approaches.name: Kahn’s algorithm for topological sort
approaches.description: Compute in-degrees, enqueue zero in-degree nodes, and build the order while checking for cycles.
approaches.why_not: DFS works too, but Kahn’s algorithm directly detects invalid course graphs by leftover nodes.

interview_qa.q: How can you detect a cycle in prerequisites?
interview_qa.a: If topological sort finishes with fewer than numCourses nodes, a cycle exists.

interview_qa.q: What is the role of indegree?
interview_qa.a: Indegree counts prerequisites remaining before a course can be taken.
